spec.name: snapback-local-safety-net
spec.scope: Local-first real-time code protection for AI-assisted development
spec.core_value: Git is for commits. This is for 'oh no, what did I just do?'

v1: Local-only by default; no code content leaves the machine
Granular recovery points with explainable PRE/POST checkpoints
non_goals_v1: First-class multi-root workspace UX (grouping, disambiguation, per-folder controls)
Rename tracking as a semantic rename (handled as delete+create)
Heavy semantic summaries on the hot path

non_negotiables: Local-only by default; cloud sync requires explicit opt-in
Performance is a feature; strict latency budgets
Degrade gracefully: if uncertain or under load, over-protect (more snapshots)
Reversibility: every SnapBack action must be undoable

behavior: Snapshots may include files from multiple workspace folders
UI may be flat (no per-folder grouping)
Timelines must not mix across distinct workspaces

v1_notes: Deletion detection is event-based (not manifest set-diff)
Tombstones store previous blob hash for recreation

system_boundary.description: Local-first checkpointing + rollback service inside VS Code extension host

external_dependencies.description: Telemetry sink for anonymized metadata only (opt-in)

level_2_containers.name: Client Layer (VSCode Extension)
responsibilities: Tap editor events with minimal overhead

level_3_components.purpose: Aggregates raw editor events into a stream suitable for burst detection
Groups events into bursts and computes features on the hot path
Converts burst features into decisions (PRE/POST), origin, reasons
Debounces and ensures at most one PRE per burst/risk window
Orchestrates manifest writes, CAS blobs, state/index/head-map updates, locking
Computes target state, diffs, applies safe WorkspaceEdit rollback plan
CAS blob storage keyed by sha256(content)
Append-only manifest storage + lookup via index
Optional async cache for UI grouping (cold path)

snapshot_manifest_v2.description: Immutable append-only record. POST manifests are deltas. PRE and PRE_ROLLBACK are pointer-only (no blobs).

files.description: Workspace-relative path -> blob ref. EMPTY for PRE/PRE_ROLLBACK.

deletions.description: Workspace-relative path -> prior blob reference for recreation. Usually included only on POST.

head_map_json_v1.description: Materialized view of HEAD only. Used for fast tombstones and fast decisions. Not the sole source of truth.

notes: Bursts group high-frequency edits into meaningful units
Running feature computation must remain on hot path

features.description: Multiple files touched in same burst window

pre_checkpoint_hot_path.trigger_condition: riskScore exceeds threshold AND no active PRE for current burst
behavior: Keep head state in memory; do not read-from-disk on hot path
For each changed doc: read bytes -> hash -> compress -> CAS store if missing
Include pending deletions from tombstone tracker
Kick optional async session cache recompute

steps: Maintain in-memory copy of head-map (relPath -> blobRef or null)
On delete event, look up prev blob from head-map and record pending deletion entry
On next POST, flush pending deletions into manifest.deletions and clear pending set

invariants: Always create PRE_ROLLBACK pointer checkpoint before changes
Apply changes via single WorkspaceEdit so editor Undo reverses it
steps: Materialize target state at targetSeq by walking parent chain and applying deltas
Compute diff between current head materialized state and target state
WorkspaceEdit: create/write/delete files to match target
Optionally write a POST manifest with trigger=rollback (prefer blob refs from target)
notes: Materialization is cold-path O(n) in number of snapshots; acceptable for v1 rollback frequency
Future optimization: periodic state checkpoints or segmented indices

single_writer_lock.requirement: All writes to manifests/state/index/head-map must hold the lock

steps: Scan snapshots directory for manifests under workspaceKey
Sort by seq if valid else timestamp; re-linearize if necessary

notes: Legacy manifests may be incorporated in rebuild/index without rewriting to v2
Option A: do not mutate user history on upgrade

commands.description: Export local diagnostics (metadata only) for support

restore_flow: undo available via editor and PRE_ROLLBACK

acceptance_criteria.description: High-velocity burst triggers PRE creation under p95 15ms without editor stutter
Every rollback is preceded by automatic PRE_ROLLBACK checkpoint
Hard crash mid-write does not permanently corrupt timeline; rebuild repairs state/index on next startup
Saving identical content twice stores one blob due to CAS deduplication
Deleting a file then restoring a prior snapshot recreates it correctly using tombstone prevBlobHash
Rollback applied via WorkspaceEdit can be undone via editor Undo (Cmd/Ctrl+Z)

known_limitations_v1: Multi-root supported but UI not optimized (flat view, no per-folder grouping)
Renames treated as delete+create (no semantic rename tracking)
Rollback target materialization is cold-path O(n) by walking manifest chain

mermaid.context_level_1:
graph TD
  User[Developer] -->|Edits code / rollbacks| VSCode[VS Code]
  VSCode -->|Extension API| SnapBack[SnapBack]
  subgraph LocalMachine
    SnapBack -->|Read/Write| LocalFs[(globalStorageUri FS)]
  end
  SnapBack -.->|Opt-in metadata only| Telemetry[PostHog]
mermaid.hot_path_sequence:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant Editor
  participant Agg as SignalAggregator
  participant Burst as BurstDetector
  participant Dec as DecisionEngine
  participant Pre as PreCheckpointMgr
  participant Store as StorageMgr
  Note over Editor,Store: Hot Path
  Editor->>Agg: change event
  Agg->>Burst: aggregated event
  Burst->>Dec: features
  Dec-->>Pre: shouldPre?
  alt High risk and no PRE
    Pre->>Store: write PRE manifest
  else No action
    Dec-->>Pre: shouldPre=false
  end
mermaid.post_path_sequence:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant Editor
  participant Dec as DecisionEngine
  participant Store as StorageMgr
  participant Blob as BlobStore
  participant Snap as SnapshotStore
  Editor->>Dec: save/burst end
  Dec->>Store: create POST
  par per changed file
    Store->>Blob: store content (hash+compress)
    Blob-->>Store: blobHash
  end
  Store->>Snap: write POST manifest (delta)
  Snap-->>Store: seq/id
